title: 14 Considerations for Outgoing Wires
body_text:
14	Considerations for Outgoing Wires
14.1	Overview of Considerations for Outgoing Wires
This chapter describes specific considerations and requirements for international outgoing wires (e.g., IBAN requirements, country-specific guidelines and instructions, and Canadian warnings, sanctions, and prohibitions). The chapter also includes cut-off times for outgoing wires and information on creating outgoing wires on Saturdays.
Follow your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs policies and procedures for processing outgoing wires. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
14.2	Considerations for International Outgoing Wires
Consider the following when sending an international outgoing wire:
The benefits of sending the wire in the currency of the destination country. This may include
ГўВҲВ’
potentially lower fees,
ГўВҲВ’
exchange rate certainty and competitiveness, and
ГўВҲВ’
no foreign conversion fees since the wire is received in the local currency.
International outgoing wires sent in Canadian dollars
ГўВҲВ’
may take longer to process than those sent in the currency of the destination country,
ГўВҲВ’
are converted to local currency by the intermediary financial institution selected when creating a wire or by the beneficiary bank if the beneficiary doesnГўВҖВҷt maintain a Canadian dollar account. Therefore, the rate of exchange is unknown.
Note:	Must include
the intermediary financial institutionГўВҖВҷs routing information.
Outgoing wires processed through an intermediary financial institution can have fees deducted from the amount of the wire. Central 1 canГўВҖВҷt control the fees that other financial institutions charge.
Once Central 1 forwards an outgoing wire to the intermediary financial institution, Central 1 no longer controls the transaction.
If the receiving financial institution discontinues business or if a foreign government is overthrown, the outgoing wire may not be completed. A recall of the outgoing wire may be unsuccessful. Recalls arenГўВҖВҷt guaranteed and are done on a best-efforts basis.
Important
:	Wire recalls, amendments, and trace requests are processed on a best-efforts basis and arenГўВҖВҷt guaranteed. Once a wire has been released by Central 1, the receiving bank must voluntarily agree to return the funds. Central 1 will make every reasonable effort to support your request, however, the receiving institution may decline, delay, or be unable to return the funds.
Due to political unrest, there may be occasions when an outgoing wire must be made in a currency other than the domestic currency of the country.
Outgoing wires are scanned against the Canadian sanctions lists. The receiving and/or correspondent financial institution may also scan the outgoing wire against its countryГўВҖВҷs sanctions list(s).
If an outgoing wire is sent to the United States or is sent in US dollars to another international destination, the funds may be frozen if a match in the OFAC sanctions list is identified. To have the funds returned, the sender must submit a license application to OFAC. OFACГўВҖВҷs License Division reviews all applications and issues or denies requests based on US foreign policy. There is no guarantee that frozen funds are returned to the sender.
14.3	IBAN Requirements
The International Bank Account Number (IBAN) is a standard means of identifying and validating account numbers in cross-border electronic payments to IBAN-regulated countries. The IBAN contains the beneficiaryГўВҖВҷs basic account number, the country code of the destination country, and IBAN check digits for validation. An IBAN improves the potential for straight-through processing.
All outgoing wires destined for IBAN-regulated countries require an IBAN, regardless of the currency or amount.
Caution
:	Failure to include the correct IBAN can result in costly charges. The recipient bank can deduct fees from a wire if it must correct an IBAN. These fees vary by financial institution and can be substantial. If the receiving financial institution canГўВҖВҷt process the wire, itГўВҖВҷs returned less any applicable fees. Central 1 passes all fees and currency conversion losses or gains to the sending financial institution.
Wires prompts users to enter an IBAN if the recipient country is IBAN regulated. Wires validates the length and starting characters and warns users if the data entered doesnГўВҖВҷt pass validation. Wires canГўВҖВҷt verify if the IBAN is correct. Wires doesnГўВҖВҷt prevent a wire from being sent if an IBAN isnГўВҖВҷt entered or is entered incorrectly. See Section 16.8, Completing the Beneficiary Section.
Tip
:	Use Form 9252, International Bank Account Number (IBAN) Countries, to determine if a destination country is IBAN-regulated.
Related Information
International Bank Account Number (IBAN) Countries,
Form 9252
14.4	Country-Specific Guidelines, Instructions, and Payment Codes
Outgoing wires sent outside of Canada may be subject to country-specific guidelines, instructions, or payment code requirements. For example, some countries require the sending financial institution to include the reason for the wire in the wire details. Other countries require the senderГўВҖВҷs government-issued identification number (e.g., driverГўВҖВҷs licence number). Wires displays these requirements to users when they enter an outgoing wire (i.e., when the user completes the Payment Details section).
Note:	Wires provides the notifications on a best-efforts basis, and they arenГўВҖВҷt guaranteed. See the Related Information section for additional information on applicable instructions, guidelines, and payment codes.
Related Information

14.5	Outgoing Wires Monitored in Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM)
Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM) system monitors outgoing wires and provides alerts for wires identified as potential fraud. Only approved wires are monitored in EFM. If an outgoing wire alerts as potential fraud in EFM, its status in the Wires application changes to ГўВҖВңPendingГўВҖВқ. Wires doesnГўВҖВҷt notify Wires Users or customers when wires are held in EFM as potential fraud.
Note:
Wires doesnГўВҖВҷt indicate why an outgoing wire has a pending status. Authorized EFM users at your financial institution can access details for wires that are under investigation for potential fraud. See the Enterprise Fraud Management Part for more information.
Authorized users at your financial institution manage outgoing wires alerted as potential fraud in EFM.
Outgoing wires identified as legitimate in EFM process as usual in the Wires application.
Outgoing wires identified as fraud in EFM are automatically cancelled in the Wires application and the wire status changes to ГўВҖВңCancelledГўВҖВқ. Your financial institution isnГўВҖВҷt debited for the transaction
. ThereГўВҖВҷs no indication why an outgoing wire was cancelled in the Wires application. However, authorized EFM users have access to the details of wires that were cancelled due to fraud.
Establish policies for managing outgoing wires alerted as potential fraud or identified as fraud (e.g., communicating internally to appropriate teams for wires that are under investigation by your fraud team and wires identified as confirmed fraud in EFM). See Section 3.3, Establishing Policies and Procedures for Outgoing Wires Monitored in Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM).
Note:	The Wires application isnГўВҖВҷt connected to your banking system. Follow your internal procedures to settle with the sender.
14.6	Tax Haven / High Risk, Sanctioned, and Prohibited Country Warnings
Wires notifies users of applicable warnings, sanctions, and prohibitions when entering the senderГўВҖВҷs country, the beneficiaryГўВҖВҷs country, or the beneficiary financial institutionГўВҖВҷs country. See Chapter 16, Creating a Regular Wire, and Chapter 17, Creating a Bank to Bank Wire.
Users can also refer to the Country-Specific Guidelines and Instructions, Form 3597. This document provides a list of applicable countries and a description of the message that appears in Wires.
Note:	Wires provides the notifications on a best-efforts basis and they arenГўВҖВҷt guaranteed. Wires doesnГўВҖВҷt warn users of sanctions that apply to an individual or entity. Refer to the Global Affairs Canada website for the most current information.
Follow your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs policies and procedures to determine whether to proceed with an outgoing wire. Considerations include, but arenГўВҖВҷt limited to, the following:
Scanning all outgoing wires against sanctions lists to identify matches of individual name, entity or organization name, and financial institution name
Handling outgoing wires if the sender or beneficiaryГўВҖВҷs name matches an entry on sanctions lists
Important
:	Wires doesnГўВҖВҷt prevent the user from completing the outgoing wire to high risk, tax haven, or sanctioned countries.
Central 1 scans all approved wires against government-provided sanctions lists, however your financial institution is responsible to identify and take subsequent action regarding wires to a sanctioned individual or entity.
If Central 1 identifies a possible match, the status of the wire changes to ГўВҖВңFrozenГўВҖВқ. Central 1 will confirm if further investigation is required within one business day. If Central 1 determines that further investigation isnГўВҖВҷt required, the status of the wire changes as follows:
From ГўВҖВңFrozenГўВҖВқ to ГўВҖВңPendingГўВҖВқ for outgoing wires directed to institutions that donГўВҖВҷt participate in Wires.
From ГўВҖВңFrozenГўВҖВқ to ГўВҖВңSentГўВҖВқ for outgoing wires directed to financial institutions that do participate in Wires.
Note:	The ГўВҖВңFrozenГўВҖВқ status also appears during the scanning process and may take several minutes to complete.
If Central 1 confirms that an outgoing wire matches an entry on the sanctions lists or further investigation is required, the wire status will change from ГўВҖВңFrozenГўВҖВқ to ГўВҖВңUnder InvestigationГўВҖВқ. Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Compliance Department will work with your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs corporate security department and any required government agency to determine the outcome of the funds.
You can view outgoing wires with a ГўВҖВңFrozenГўВҖВқ or ГўВҖВңUnder InvestigationГўВҖВқ status in Wires, but you canГўВҖВҷt cancel the wire. Your financial institution is debited for the transactions, but the funds are held at Central 1 until the investigation concludes.
Follow your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs policies and procedures for outgoing wires that are frozen or under investigation. Your financial institution must establish a policy to ensure that staff donГўВҖВҷt alert the individual when an incoming or outgoing wire is frozen or under investigation. If asked, advise the individual that the wire is pending but donГўВҖВҷt divulge any information that may compromise an investigation.
PCMLTFA
prohibits the disclosure of information on transactions that will be or are reported, regardless of whether a criminal investigation has begun. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
Note:	Wires sent outside of Canada can be scanned by the receiving financial institution, intermediary financial institution(s), and/or correspondent financial institution against their own sanctions lists. As a result, funds can be frozen in another jurisdiction even if the wire was successfully processed in Wires.
Related Information
Country-Specific Guidelines and Instructions,
Form 3597
14.7	Cut-off Times for Outgoing Wires
Approve wires before the applicable cut-off time to ensure they are processed and settled with CentralВ 1 (not the destination) the same business day. All cut-off times are listed in Eastern Time (ET) and are effective on business days (Monday to Friday, excluding statutory holidays). Wires that arenГўВҖВҷt processed the same day are processed and settled the next business day.
Note:	Outgoing wires are monitored in Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Enterprise Fraud Management system and are scanned against sanctions lists. If a wire is alerted as potential fraud in EFM, or as a possible compliance list match, the wire is held for investigation. See Section 14.5, Outgoing Wires Monitored in Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM) and Section 14.6, Tax Haven / High Risk, Sanctioned, and Prohibited Country Warnings, for more information.
Foreign dollar wires created after the cut-off time on a business day are settled using the exchange rate provided in Wires. This provides rate certainty for the customer as Central 1 wonГўВҖВҷt need to process a rate adjustment to your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs Central 1 account on the following day.
Outgoing Wire Cut-off Times
Sending Wire To
Currency
Cut-off Time (Eastern Time)
Completion Date
A financial institution that participates in Wires

7:00 pm ET
Note: Wires sent after 6:00 pm ET requiring a manual compliance review wonГўВҖВҷt be released until the morning of the following business day.
Generally, the same day. Consider the time difference between provinces. Some wires may not be credited to the beneficiary until the following business day.
A financial institution in Canada that doesnГўВҖВҷt participate in Wires
All currencies
5:15 pm ET
Generally, the same day.
A financial institution in the US
All currencies
5:15 pm ET
Generally, the same day or the following day.
A financial institution in a foreign country (other than the US)
All currencies
5:15 pm ET
Generally, two to three business days.
14.8	Outgoing Wires Created on Saturdays
Wires created on a Saturday and destined for another financial institution that participates in Wires are available in the receiving financial institutionГўВҖВҷs pending incoming wires the following business day (provided the transaction is approved and passes sanctions lists scans). Settlement is also processed the following business day.
Outgoing wires destined for financial institutions that donГўВҖВҷt participate in Wires can be created on Saturdays at a guaranteed rate. The foreign exchange rate in Wires is the rate used for settlement. Rates arenГўВҖВҷt live on Saturday, theyГўВҖВҷre held from Friday through to Sunday. Outgoing wires entered on Saturdays are sent on the following business day. Settlement is processed the following business day.
END
